Fibonacci levels help you see where momentum pauses by mapping potential areas of support & resistance using ratios from the Fib sequence.  

I find them useful because strong trends often consolidate at these levels before continuing higher & this list reflects names consolidating between the 0.382  & 0.618 extension levels.

Money managers have abandoned positioning for $200 oil ... does this now increase the risk of $200 oil?
-----
The CFTC was closed today, so the Commitment of Traders report for June 16 will be released on Monday.

But the Intercontinental Exchange released its data for London's Brent Crude Oil Contract for June 16 today.

Here is the net (long-short) for managed money (aka "money managers").

The chart starts on April 30, 2020 (beginning of the post-COVID recovery).

In the last four weeks, these managers went from a fairly long position (see the May 19 position in the bottom panel) to a fairly low net long position (June 16). Note that these managers are rarely net short (below zero).

@CommodMkt @Rory_Johnston

Careful here. Oversupply leads to lower prices, but it does not cause deflation. 

Lower prices and deflation are not the same thing. 

The confusion lies in the fact that  you can have lower prices without having deflation, but you cannot have deflation without lower prices.
